{"society_power": 4.5, "state_power": 2.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 1.0, "key_event": "Federal Government formed; territory reclaimed from al-Shabaab", "reasoning": "The 2012 Federal Government, provisional constitution, recapture of Mogadishu/Kismayo, and orderly succession modestly rebuilt central state capacity from near-collapse, though it remained weak and externally dependent with al-Shabaab as a rival coercive actor. Society power held steady: clan structures and remittance-driven economic autonomy stayed strong but were neither newly empowered nor suppressed."}
